content.s1_claims_discipline_internal:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TAGS: mitochondria, energy, ATP, fatigue, CoQ10, PQQ, cellular energy, electron transport chain, NAD+, NADH, krebs cycle, TCA, oxidative phosphorylation, biogenesis, mitophagy, L-carnitine, alpha lipoic acid, ALA, ribose, creatine, tired, exhausted, no energy, chronic fatigue, CFS, ME/CFS, mitochondrial, energy production, NAD, mitochondrial dysfunction, low energy, bioenergetics, PGC-1alpha, reactive oxygen species ROS, oxidative stress mitochondria, mitochondrial membrane potential, complex I II III IV ETC, uncoupling protein UCP, mitophagy clearance, mitochondrial fission fusion, mtDNA, heteroplasmy, mitochondrial disease, mitochondrial mass, d-ribose ATP
# CATEGORY: Systems
# USE FOR: Fatigue, low energy, mitochondrial support, cellular energy questions, chronic fatigue
# COUPLED_SYSTEMS: 08_LONGEVITY_NAD, 09_HPA_ADRENAL, 14_SLEEP, 06_METABOLIC, 32_IV_THERAPIES, 15_COGNITIVE
# RED_FLAGS: Unexplained severe fatigue + weight loss + night sweats (rule out malignancy), sudden extreme fatigue + chest pain (cardiac)
# TRIAGE_QUESTIONS: How long has the fatigue lasted? Any other symptoms (weight loss, night sweats, pain)? Currently on any medications?
# IV_ROLE: IV NAD+ is appropriate for mitochondrial support in Track B patients with confirmed energy deficit or as a loading phase. It is NOT first-line for general fatigue — rule out thyroid, iron, sleep, adrenals first. Oral CoQ10, B vitamins, D-ribose, and creatine are first-line. IV is a booster, not a substitute for root cause investigation.
# INTERVENTION_PRIORITY: Root cause identification → Lifestyle (exercise, sleep) → Oral supplements (CoQ10, B vitamins, NAD+ precursors) → IV NAD+ for loading/boost → Ongoing oral maintenance
# TELECONSULT_LIMITS: CAN do remotely: full anamnesis, fatigue assessment, lifestyle plan, supplement protocol, lab ordering, follow-up. CANNOT do remotely: exercise capacity testing, cardiopulmonary assessment. Note: most mitochondrial support is lifestyle+oral — highly teleconsult-compatible.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# MITOCHONDRIAL HEALTH
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 === SECTION 1: CLAIMS DISCIPLINE [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## MITOCHONDRIA: YOUR CELLULAR POWER PLANTS

Inside almost every cell in your body are tiny structures called mitochondria.
Their primary job: **produce ATP (adenosine triphosphate) — the energy currency 
that powers everything you do.**

**Every thought, every heartbeat, every movement, every immune response, every 
repair process — all require ATP.**

You have trillions of mitochondria. High-energy organs have the most:
- Brain: 2 million mitochondria per neuron
- Heart: 5,000 mitochondria per heart muscle cell
- Muscles, liver, kidneys: Also very mitochondria-dense

**When mitochondria fail, energy production drops, and everything fails.**

---

## WHY MITOCHONDRIAL HEALTH MATTERS

| Healthy Mitochondria | Dysfunctional Mitochondria |
|---------------------|---------------------------|
| Abundant energy | Chronic fatigue |
| Mental clarity | Brain fog |
| Strong muscles | Weakness |
| Healthy aging | Accelerated aging |
| Disease resistance | Chronic disease |
| Good mood | Depression |
| Weight management | Metabolic dysfunction |

**Mitochondrial dysfunction is implicated in:**
- Chronic fatigue syndrome / ME
- Fibromyalgia
- Parkinson's and Alzheimer's disease
- Heart failure
- Diabetes
- Cancer
- Aging itself
- Long COVID

---

## HOW MITOCHONDRIA PRODUCE ENERGY

A simplified version of the magic:

```
FOOD (Glucose, Fats)
        │
        ▼
    Broken down in digestion
        │
        ▼
    Enters cells
        │
        ▼
    MITOCHONDRIA
        │
    ┌───┴───┐
    │       │
    ▼       ▼
Krebs    Electron
Cycle    Transport
         Chain (ETC)
    │       │
    └───┬───┘
        │
        ▼
    ATP PRODUCED
    (36 ATP per glucose)
        │
        ▼
    ENERGY FOR LIFE
```

**Key players:**
- **NAD+** — Essential electron carrier (see 08_LONGEVITY_NAD)
- **CoQ10** — Shuttles electrons in the ETC
- **Oxygen** — Final electron acceptor
- **B vitamins** — Cofactors at multiple steps
- **Magnesium** — ATP exists as Mg-ATP

**Byproducts:**
- Water (good)
- CO2 (exhaled)
- Reactive Oxygen Species (ROS) — free radicals (problematic if excessive)

---

## WHAT DAMAGES MITOCHONDRIA?

| Threat | Mechanism |
|--------|-----------|
| **Oxidative stress** | ROS damage mtDNA and membranes |
| **Nutrient deficiencies** | Can't build or run the machinery |
| **Toxins** | Direct mitochondrial poisons |
| **Chronic inflammation** | Impairs function |
| **Chronic stress** | Cortisol damages mitochondria |
| **Poor sleep** | Impairs repair and turnover |
| **Sedentary lifestyle** | Mitochondria atrophy without use |
| **Excess calories/sugar** | Overwhelms system, increases ROS |
| **Medications** | Statins, metformin, antibiotics can impair |
| **Aging** | mtDNA mutations accumulate |
| **Infections** | Especially chronic viral (EBV, COVID) |
| **Mycotoxins (mold)** | Direct mitochondrial poisons |

---

## MITOCHONDRIAL TOXINS TO KNOW

Some substances directly poison mitochondria:

**Mycotoxins (mold toxins):**
- Ochratoxin A — inhibits ATP production
- Aflatoxin — damages mtDNA
- Trichothecenes — disrupts ETC
- Gliotoxin — mitochondrial apoptosis

*If you have unexplained fatigue and mold exposure history, this is critical.*
See: 23_MOLD_CIRS

**Heavy metals:**
- Mercury — binds sulfhydryl groups, disrupts ETC
- Lead — impairs multiple enzymes
- Arsenic — uncouples oxidative phosphorylation

**Medications:**
- Statins — deplete CoQ10 (essential for ETC)
- Metformin — inhibits Complex I (can be therapeutic at low dose)
- Fluoroquinolone antibiotics — mtDNA damage
- Acetaminophen (high dose) — depletes glutathione

**Other:**
- Glyphosate (herbicide) — disrupts cellular energy
- Cyanide — classic mitochondrial poison
- Alcohol (chronic excess) — mitochondrial damage

---

## SIGNS OF MITOCHONDRIAL DYSFUNCTION

- Persistent, unexplained fatigue
- Post-exertional malaise (exhaustion after activity)
- Exercise intolerance
- Brain fog, cognitive difficulties
- Muscle weakness or pain
- Cold intolerance
- Slow recovery from illness or exertion
- Premature aging
- Multiple system involvement

---

## SUPPORTING MITOCHONDRIAL HEALTH

### Key Nutrients

| Nutrient | Role | Dose Range |
|----------|------|------------|
| **CoQ10 (Ubiquinol)** | Electron carrier in ETC | 100-300mg (ubiquinol preferred 40+) |
| **NAD+ precursors** | Essential electron carrier | NMN 250-500mg or NR |
| **PQQ** | Stimulates new mitochondria | 10-20mg |
| **Alpha-lipoic acid** | Antioxidant, regenerates others | 300-600mg (R-form preferred) |
| **Acetyl-L-Carnitine** | Shuttles fatty acids into mitochondria | 500-2000mg |
| **B vitamins** | Cofactors throughout | B-complex with active forms |
| **Magnesium** | ATP exists as Mg-ATP | 400-600mg |
| **D-Ribose** | ATP backbone, rebuilds ATP pools | 5g 2-3x/day |
| **Creatine** | Phosphocreatine buffer | 3-5g/day |

### Lifestyle Factors

**Exercise (the most powerful intervention):**
- Stimulates mitochondrial biogenesis (new mitochondria!)
- Improves mitochondrial efficiency
- Activates PGC-1α (master regulator)
- Best: Zone 2 cardio + HIIT + resistance training

**Cold exposure:**
- Activates brown fat (mitochondria-dense)
- Stimulates PGC-1α
- Cold showers, cold plunge, cryotherapy

**Fasting / Time-restricted eating:**
- Activates AMPK (mitochondrial biogenesis)
- Triggers autophagy (clears damaged mitochondria)
- Improves metabolic flexibility

**Sleep:**
- Mitochondrial repair happens during sleep
- Poor sleep = impaired mitochondrial function

**Sunlight / Red light:**
- Red/near-infrared light stimulates cytochrome c oxidase
- Improves mitochondrial function (photobiomodulation)
- See 34_BIOPHYSICAL_THERAPIES

### Remove Mitochondrial Toxins

- Address mold exposure (see 23_MOLD_CIRS)
- Consider heavy metal testing if suspicious
- Minimize unnecessary medications
- Reduce toxin exposure in environment

---

## SPECIAL SITUATION: CHRONIC FATIGUE SYNDROME (CFS/ME)

Mitochondrial dysfunction is central to CFS/ME.

**Key features:**
- Post-exertional malaise (PEM) — worsening 24-72h after exertion
- Cellular energy deficit
- Inability to meet energy demands

**Critical understanding:**
**CFS is NOT deconditioning. Pushing through makes it WORSE.**

**Approach:**
1. **Pacing** — Stay within energy envelope
2. **Rebuild mitochondria gradually** — Don't overstimulate
3. **Address underlying causes** — Infections, mold, gut, HPA
4. **Support mitochondria** — CoQ10, NAD+, B vitamins, D-ribose
5. **NO aggressive exercise** — Unlike other conditions

See 05_EXERCISE_MOVEMENT for CFS-specific guidance.

**Protocol:**
- CoQ10 200-400mg
- D-Ribose 5g 3x/day
- ALCAR 1000mg
- B vitamins (active forms)
- NAD+ support (oral or IV)
- PQQ 20mg
- Magnesium 400-600mg
- Pacing (non-negotiable)

---

## MITOCHONDRIA AND AGING

**The mitochondrial theory of aging:**

```
AGING PROCESS

mtDNA damage accumulates
        │
        ▼
Mitochondrial function declines
        │
        ▼
ATP production drops
        │
        ▼
Cells can't maintain function
        │
        ▼
Tissue/organ dysfunction
        │
        ▼
AGING & AGE-RELATED DISEASE
```

**Why mitochondria are vulnerable:**
- mtDNA has no protective histones
- mtDNA near ROS production (gets damaged first)
- Limited repair mechanisms
- mtDNA mutations accumulate over time

**Anti-aging implication:**
Protecting and regenerating mitochondria is central to longevity.
See: 08_LONGEVITY_NAD

---

## TESTING MITOCHONDRIAL FUNCTION

**Indirect markers:**

| Test | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| Lactate (fasting) | Elevated suggests mitochondrial dysfunction |
| Pyruvate | Lactate/pyruvate ratio informative |
| Organic acids (OAT) | Krebs cycle intermediates, dysfunction patterns |
| CoQ10 level | Deficiency indicates need |
| Carnitine profile | Transport capacity |
| VO2 max | Functional mitochondrial capacity |
| HRV | Autonomic/energy status |

**Direct testing:**
- Muscle biopsy (gold standard, invasive)
- mtDNA sequencing (genetic mitochondrial disease)

**Functional assessment:**
- How many stairs can you climb?
- Do you crash after exertion?
- Energy patterns through the day?

---

 === SECTION 2: PATIENT COMMUNICATION [PATIENT_OK] ===
## ELECTRON TRANSPORT CHAIN — DETAILED

```
INNER MITOCHONDRIAL MEMBRANE

                    Intermembrane space (high H+)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    │           │           │           │               │
    │           │           │           │               │
    ▼           ▼           ▼           ▼               ▼
COMPLEX I   COMPLEX II  COMPLEX III  COMPLEX IV    ATP SYNTHASE
  (NADH      (Succinate   (Cyt bc1)   (Cyt c        (Complex V)
Dehydrog)    Dehydrog)               oxidase)
    │           │           │           │               │
    │     CoQ10 ←┘           │           │               │
    └────────→ CoQ10 ────────┘           │               │
                    │                    │               │
                    └───→ Cyt c ─────────┘               │
                                         │               │
                                    O2 → H2O             │
                                                         │
                         H+ flows back in ───────────────┘
                                    │
                                    ▼
                               ATP PRODUCED
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
                         Matrix (low H+)


NADH → Complex I → CoQ10 → Complex III → Cytochrome c → Complex IV → O2 → H2O
FADH2 → Complex II → CoQ10 → (continues same pathway)

H+ pumped out at Complex I, III, IV
H+ flows back through ATP Synthase → ATP generated
```

**Where things go wrong:**

| Complex | Inhibited By | Supported By |
|---------|-------------|--------------|
| Complex I | Metformin, rotenone, mycotoxins | NAD+, riboflavin |
| Complex II | Malonate, some drugs | FAD (from riboflavin), CoQ10 |
| Complex III | Antimycin A, mold toxins | CoQ10 |
| Complex IV | Cyanide, carbon monoxide | Copper, iron, oxygen |
| ATP Synthase | Oligomycin | Magnesium (ATP = Mg-ATP) |

## MITOCHONDRIAL BIOGENESIS PATHWAYS

```
STIMULI FOR BIOGENESIS:

Exercise (AMPK, Ca²⁺) ─────┐
                          │
Cold exposure ────────────┼───→ PGC-1α ACTIVATION
                          │      (Master regulator)
Fasting/Caloric restriction─┤           │
                          │           ▼
NAD+/Sirtuins ────────────┤    ┌──────────────────────┐
                          │    │ NRF1, NRF2           │
ROS (mild) ───────────────┘    │ (Nuclear factors)    │
                               │                      │
                               │ TFAM                 │
                               │ (Mitochondrial TF)   │
                               └──────────┬───────────┘
                                          │
                                          ▼
                          ┌───────────────────────────────┐
                          │ MITOCHONDRIAL BIOGENESIS      │
                          │ • mtDNA replication           │
                          │ • New ETC complex synthesis   │
                          │ • New mitochondrial membranes │
                          │ • MORE MITOCHONDRIA           │
                          └───────────────────────────────┘
```

## MITOPHAGY — QUALITY CONTROL

```
MITOPHAGY: Selective autophagy of damaged mitochondria

Damaged mitochondria (low membrane potential, ROS)
        │
        ▼
PINK1 accumulates on outer membrane
        │
        ▼
Recruits Parkin (E3 ubiquitin ligase)
        │
        ▼
Ubiquitination of outer membrane proteins
        │
        ▼
Recognition by autophagy machinery
        │
        ▼
Engulfment by autophagosome
        │
        ▼
Fusion with lysosome
        │
        ▼
DEGRADATION and RECYCLING

Stimulated by:
• Fasting
• Exercise
• NAD+ (through sirtuins)
• Rapamycin
• Urolithin A (new compound)
• Spermidine
```

## CLINICAL PROTOCOLS — DETAILED

### Mitochondrial Restoration Protocol (Standard)

**Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-4)**

| Supplement | Dose | Timing | Purpose |
|------------|------|--------|---------|
| CoQ10 (Ubiquinol) | 200mg | With fat-containing meal | ETC support |
| Magnesium glycinate | 400mg | Evening | ATP cofactor |
| B-Complex (active forms) | Per label | Morning | Krebs/ETC cofactors |
| Omega-3 (EPA/DHA) | 2g | With meals | Membrane integrity |
| Vitamin D | Per level | Morning | Mitochondrial function |

Lifestyle:
- Zone 2 exercise 30 min 4x/week (if tolerated)
- Sleep optimization
- Remove obvious toxins

**Phase 2: Enhancement (Weeks 5-12)**

Add:
| Supplement | Dose | Purpose |
|------------|------|---------|
| PQQ | 20mg | Biogenesis |
| Alpha-lipoic acid (R-form) | 300-600mg | Antioxidant, ETC |
| Acetyl-L-Carnitine | 1000mg | Fatty acid transport |
| NAD+ support (NMN or NR) | 250-500mg | Central energy molecule |

Lifestyle:
- Add HIIT 1x/week (if tolerated)
- Cold exposure (cold showers, etc.)
- Consider TRE (time-restricted eating)

**Phase 3: Optimization (Month 3+)**

Add as indicated:
| Supplement | Dose | Purpose |
|------------|------|---------|
| Creatine | 3-5g | Phosphocreatine buffer |
| D-Ribose | 5g 1-2x | ATP rebuilding |
| Urolithin A | 500-1000mg | Mitophagy (emerging) |

Consider:
- NAD+ IV therapy (see 08_LONGEVITY_NAD)
- Red light therapy
- HBOT (for specific indications)

### CFS/ME Mitochondrial Protocol

**CRITICAL DIFFERENCE: Do NOT push exercise. Pacing is essential.**

**Phase 1: Gentle Support (Weeks 1-8)**

| Supplement | Dose | Notes |
|------------|------|-------|
| CoQ10 (Ubiquinol) | 200-400mg | Higher dose for CFS |
| D-Ribose | 5g 3x/day | ATP pool rebuilding |
| Magnesium glycinate | 400-600mg | Often deficient |
| B-Complex (active) | Per label | Cautious if sensitive |
| NADH (sublingual) | 10-20mg | Alternative NAD+ support |

Lifestyle:
- Strict pacing (stay in energy envelope)
- Rest before crashing
- Gentle stretching only
- Sleep prioritization (even if non-restorative)

**Phase 2: Gradual Build (Weeks 9-24)**

Add carefully:
| Supplement | Dose | Notes |
|------------|------|-------|
| Acetyl-L-Carnitine | 500-1000mg | Start low |
| PQQ | 10-20mg | Biogenesis support |
| Alpha-lipoic acid | 300mg | Antioxidant |
| NMN | 250mg | NAD+ (oral, gentle) |

Consider:
- NAD+ IV (start 250mg, gentle pace)
- Red light therapy (supportive, non-depleting)

**Phase 3: Expansion (Months 6+)**

- Very gradual activity increase (5-10% per week max)
- Monitor for PEM (any worsening = back off)
- May take 1-2 years for significant improvement
- Address underlying causes (see below)

**Underlying Causes to Investigate:**

| Cause | Testing | Treatment File |
|-------|---------|----------------|
| Mold/CIRS | Mycotoxins, markers | 23_MOLD_CIRS |
| Chronic infection | Lyme, EBV, etc. | 24_CHRONIC_INFECTIONS |
| Gut dysfunction | GI-MAP, SIBO | 12_GUT |
| HPA dysfunction | Cortisol, DUTCH | 09_HPA_ADRENAL |
| Autonomic | HRV, tilt table | 10_AUTONOMIC_VAGAL |

### Statin-Induced Mitochondrial Support

Statins inhibit HMG-CoA reductase → depletes CoQ10

| Supplement | Dose | Purpose |
|------------|------|---------|
| CoQ10 (Ubiquinol) | 200-400mg | Repletes depletion |
| D-Ribose | 5g 2x/day | ATP support |
| Magnesium | 400mg | Muscle function |
| Vitamin D | Per level | Often low with statin use |
| Omega-3 | 2g | Membrane support |

*Consider whether statin is truly necessary — discuss with prescriber*

### Neurodegenerative Support (Adjunctive)

Brain is highly mitochondria-dependent.

| Supplement | Dose | Notes |
|------------|------|-------|
| CoQ10 (Ubiquinol) | 300-600mg | High dose (poor BBB penetration) |
| PQQ | 20mg | Neuroprotective, biogenesis |
| Acetyl-L-Carnitine | 1500-3000mg | Crosses BBB, brain mitochondria |
| Alpha-lipoic acid (R) | 600mg | Antioxidant, crosses BBB |
| NAD+ support | NMN 500mg | Neuronal energy |
| Creatine | 5g | Brain energy buffer |
| Lion's Mane | 1-2g | NGF support |
| Phosphatidylserine | 300mg | Brain membrane |

See: 15_COGNITIVE for full cognitive protocol

### Long COVID Mitochondrial Protocol

Evidence suggests mitochondrial dysfunction in Long COVID.

| Supplement | Dose | Purpose |
|------------|------|---------|
| CoQ10 | 300mg | ETC support |
| NAD+ support | NMN 250-500mg | Energy restoration |
| D-Ribose | 5g 3x/day | ATP rebuilding |
| PQQ | 20mg | Biogenesis |
| Alpha-lipoic acid | 600mg | Antioxidant |
| Omega-3 | 2-3g | Anti-inflammatory, membranes |
| Magnesium | 400mg | Cofactor |
| Vitamin D | Optimize 50-80 | Immune, mitochondrial |

Lifestyle:
- Pacing (similar to CFS approach)
- Gradual return to activity
- Address dysautonomia if present
- See 10_AUTONOMIC_VAGAL

Consider:
- NAD+ IV therapy
- HBOT (emerging evidence)
- Red light therapy

## SUPPORTING TESTS AND MARKERS

### Organic Acids Test (OAT) — Mitochondrial Markers

| Marker | Elevated Suggests |
|--------|-------------------|
| Lactate | Mitochondrial dysfunction, anaerobic metabolism |
| Pyruvate | Less specific |
| Lactate/Pyruvate ratio | >20 suggests mitochondrial issue |
| Succinate | Complex II issue |
| Fumarate | Krebs cycle dysfunction |
| Malate | Krebs cycle dysfunction |
| Citrate | Krebs cycle entry |
| α-ketoglutarate | Krebs cycle |
| Ethylmalonate | Carnitine/fatty acid oxidation issue |
| Suberate, Adipate | β-oxidation defects |

### Other Relevant Testing

| Test | Relevance |
|------|-----------|
| Fasting lactate | >2 mmol/L suggests dysfunction |
| CoQ10 (blood) | Assess levels |
| Carnitine (total, free) | Transport capacity |
| Acylcarnitine profile | Fatty acid oxidation |
| MMA (methylmalonic acid) | B12 functional status |
| Homocysteine | Methylation affecting mito |
| VO2 max | Functional capacity |
| HRV | Energy/autonomic status |

---

 === SECTION 3: PHARMACOKINETICS & DYNAMICS (ADME) [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## MITOCHONDRIAL SUPPORT STACK

**Foundation (Everyone):**
| Supplement | Dose |
|------------|------|
| CoQ10 (Ubiquinol) | 100-200mg |
| Magnesium | 400mg |
| B-Complex (active) | Per label |
| Omega-3 | 2g |

**Enhanced (Fatigue, Optimization):**
| Supplement | Dose |
|------------|------|
| CoQ10 (Ubiquinol) | 200-400mg |
| PQQ | 10-20mg |
| Alpha-lipoic acid (R) | 300-600mg |
| Acetyl-L-Carnitine | 500-1500mg |
| NAD+ (NMN or NR) | 250-500mg |

**Intensive (CFS, Significant Dysfunction):**
Add:
| Supplement | Dose |
|------------|------|
| D-Ribose | 5g 2-3x/day |
| Creatine | 3-5g |
| CoQ10 | Up to 600mg |
| NAD+ IV | Per protocol |

## LIFESTYLE FOR MITOCHONDRIA

| Intervention | Effect |
|--------------|--------|
| Zone 2 cardio | Mitochondrial biogenesis |
| HIIT | Powerful biogenesis stimulus |
| Resistance training | Muscle mitochondria |
| Cold exposure | Brown fat, PGC-1α |
| Fasting/TRE | AMPK, autophagy, mitophagy |
| Sleep | Repair and turnover |
| Red light therapy | Stimulates Complex IV |

## MITOCHONDRIAL TOXINS TO AVOID/ADDRESS

- Mold/Mycotoxins (see 23_MOLD_CIRS)
- Heavy metals
- Excess alcohol
- Unnecessary medications (statins, fluoroquinolones)
- Pesticides/glyphosate

## CFS/ME KEY POINTS

- **Pacing is essential** — Don't push
- **PEM is real** — Not deconditioning
- **Heart rate monitoring** — Stay below threshold
- **Gradual, very gradual** — 5-10% increase max
- **Address root causes** — Mold, infections, etc.

## TESTING

| Simple | Advanced |
|--------|----------|
| Fasting lactate | Organic acids (OAT) |
| CoQ10 level | Acylcarnitine profile |
| VO2 max / fitness | Muscle biopsy (rare) |
| HRV | mtDNA sequencing (genetic) |

---
